QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_75

question.text: Under the key personnel holding restrictions, which of the following is included in the definition of "relative"?

answer.text: Spouse, parents (both sides), siblings (both sides), children and their spouses, and HUF
Only persons living in the same household

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The definition of "relative" for key personnel holding restrictions is broad and includes: spouse, parents from both sides (including in-laws), siblings from both sides, children and their spouses, and Hindu Undivided Family (HUF). This expansive definition ensures that indirect holdings through family members are captured. **The Trap:** "Only spouse and children" is too narrow — the definition extends to parents and siblings from BOTH sides (i.e., including the spouse's family). "Persons living in the same household" is a different legal concept (used in some other regulations) and not the standard here. **Cross-Exam Context:** The definition of "relative" varies across regulations — the MB regulation uses a broader definition than some other SEBI regulations. Compare with the Companies Act definition tested in corporate governance modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When conducting pre-mandate conflict checks, map the full family tree of all key personnel including in-laws — the "both sides" requirement catches relationships that are often overlooked.
